behavior_surfaces: user settings env persistence via settings.env
server settings env diff and active-session broadcast
SDK update_environment_variables control message set and unset semantics
running CLI process environment refresh for future runtime reads and child processes
generated_surfaces: desktop/dist generated by bun run check:desktop build and not intended as a source change
desktop preview server on http://127.0.0.1:4173 for local smoke verification
state_contracts: Desktop stores hydrate user-defined Agent environment variables from settings.env rather than a new persistence key.
Saving environment variables through desktop calls PUT /api/settings/user with env and preserves unknown user settings fields through the existing settings service merge path.
Server env sync computes only added, changed, and removed keys; removed keys are sent as null.
update_environment_variables accepts string values to set variables and null values to unset variables.
Runtime env updates apply to the running CLI process and future child processes, but do not mutate environment blocks already copied by external subprocesses.

verification.evidence: Failed before implementation because ConversationService lacked updateEnvironmentVariables; passed after runtime broadcast methods were added.
Failed before implementation because settings env changes did not sync active sessions; passed after env diff sync was added.

verification.evidence: Failed before implementation because agentEnvironmentVariables state was missing; passed after store hydration and persistence were added.
StructuredIO set and unset runtime environment variables from update_environment_variables messages.

user_decisions: User requested a desktop Settings environment-variable manager for Agent dependencies.
User clarified that already-running Agent sessions should receive new env values in real time.
confidence_notes: Explorer handoff identified existing settings.env and update_environment_variables protocol, avoiding a duplicate persistence shape.
Focused tests cover desktop persistence, server active-session broadcast, and CLI runtime application.
Live propagation is bounded to the running CLI process environment and future child processes, which matches what the existing control channel can safely guarantee.

excluded: Mutating machine-wide or shell profile environment variables
Logging or diagnostic display of secret values
Provider, MCP, Skills, OAuth, or adapter configuration rewrites
OS-level mutation of already-started external subprocess environment blocks
